priority_basis: The canonical queue contains no active frontier_research item. The latest owner-scoped handoff, R-026, explicitly required integrating capability negotiation into the IndexedDB acceptance ledger before browser interoperability testing.
action: Integrate capability-negotiated IndexedDB transactions into the acceptance ledger and preserve capability evidence separately from persistence claims.
sources_inspected: W3C Indexed Database API 3.0 Working Draft
finding: A single TypeError-only capability-negotiation path makes the replay ledger compatible with engines that reject transaction options without masking lifecycle failures. Requested and observed durability, options support, and fallback reason must remain distinct from a persistence claim.
verification: 3 focused deterministic Node tests passed, 0 failed, using a source-equivalent integration prototype and synthetic IndexedDB transaction doubles.
handoff: Run the committed tests in CI, then build a real-browser interoperability harness for Safari, Chromium, and Firefox covering two-tab contention, bfcache restore, forced close, quota pressure, and abrupt termination. Keep options support, transaction completion, and persistence evidence as separate fields.
